title: build fails when using glob in --from COPY statement
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**
Build fails if using glob inside a COPY statement which uses the --from flag.

Dockerfile used:
```dockerfile
FROM scratch

COPY --from=busybox:1.32.0 /lib* /lib
```

Output:
```console
$ buildah build .
STEP 1/2: FROM scratch
STEP 2/2: COPY --from=busybox:1.32.0 /lib* /lib
error building at STEP "COPY --from=busybox:1.32.0 /lib* /lib": checking on sources under "/home/ludi/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/7c289aa153a23d4ccef6170ae3be34c9ba11a5a1bcb1b90484f815d835cfa439/merged": Rel: can't make  relative to /home/ludi/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/7c289aa153a23d4ccef6170ae3be34c9ba11a5a1bcb1b90484f815d835cfa439/merged; copier: stat: ["/lib*"]: no such file or directory
```

I should mention, that /lib does not exist inside busybox.

docker successfully builds the image:
```console
$ docker build .
[+] Building 1.0s (5/5) FINISHED
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                                                                                                   0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 97B                                                                                                                                                    0.0s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                                                                                                      0.0s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                                                                                                        0.0s
 => CACHED FROM docker.io/library/busybox:1.32.0                                                                                                                                       0.0s
 => => resolve docker.io/library/busybox:1.32.0                                                                                                                                        0.9s
 => [stage-0 1/1] COPY --from=busybox:1.32.0 /lib* /lib                                                                                                                                0.0s
 => exporting to image                                                                                                                                                                 0.0s
 => => exporting layers                                                                                                                                                                0.0s
 => => writing image sha256:5936e8cc7b33544ea3dd84967dbb7aafe8bf79831239685aab5627ad5ec60b84                                                                                           0.0s
```
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. use Dockerfile
2. run `buildah build .`
3. buildah will fail


**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```console
$ rpm -q buildah
buildah-1.26.0-2.fc36.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.26.0
Go Version:      go1.18
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.0
image Version:   5.21.1
Git Commit:
Built:           Wed May  4 21:49:14 2022
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

Running inside WSL with fedora rootfilesystem

```
Fedora release 36 (Thirty Six)
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="36 (Container Image Prerelease)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=36
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f36"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora Linux 36 (Container Image Prerelease)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:36"
HOME_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora/f36/system-administrators-guide/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://ask.fedoraproject.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugzilla.redhat.com/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=36
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=36
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="Container Image"
VARIANT_ID=container
Fedora release 36 (Thirty Six)
Fedora release 36 (Thirty Six)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux Fullforce 5.10.16.3-microsoft-standard-WSL2 #1 SMP Fri Apr 2 22:23:49 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
does not exist
```

comments.body: If I think about it, buildah is doing the right thing. If a path cannot be found inside the image, the builder should error out. Not sure why buildkit is behaving like the above.

When building without buildkit, docker produces the expected error:

```console
$ DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0 docker build -f /tmp/Dockerfile /tmp
Sending build context to Docker daemon  368.1kB
Step 1/2 : FROM busybox
 ---> 3fb5cabb6469
Step 2/2 : COPY --from=busybox:1.32.0 /lib* /lib
1.32.0: Pulling from library/busybox
ea97eb0eb3ec: Already exists
Digest: sha256:bde48e1751173b709090c2539fdf12d6ba64e88ec7a4301591227ce925f3c678
Status: Downloaded newer image for busybox:1.32.0
COPY failed: no source files were specified
```

I'll open an issue in the buildkit repository.
